andy-reid.name: Andy Reid
drama_notes: Won 3 of the last 6 Super Bowls with Mahomes — building a dynasty as we speak.
Spent 14 years in Philadelphia — ran 1 Super Bowl appearance (loss to Patriots) before getting fired.
His son Garrett, a Chiefs assistant coach, was involved in a 2024 DUI crash — the franchise navigated that quietly.
Mustache is a brand. Hawaiian shirts at training camp are a brand. Cheeseburger memes are a brand.
headlines: All-time playoff wins leader for active coaches.
3x Super Bowl champion (LIV, LVII, LVIII).
First coach to win back-to-back Super Bowls in the 21st century (LVII + LVIII).
memes: The 'Big Red' cheeseburger memes — every Chiefs win = Andy gets a burger.
His perfectly-timed challenge flag throws.
Falling off the parade float at the LVII celebration.
Andy Reid's mustache as its own social media account.
off_field: From Los Angeles — went to BYU.
Coached Brett Favre, Donovan McNabb, Michael Vick, Patrick Mahomes — every era of QB.
Famously kind to assistants — coaching tree is enormous (Doug Pederson, Matt Nagy, John Harbaugh).
running_jokes: He never met a clock he couldn't mismanage — until Mahomes bailed him out.
His play sheets are the size of a dinner menu.
The post-game cheeseburger is canon.

sean-mcvay.name: Sean McVay
drama_notes: Youngest head coach to win a Super Bowl in modern NFL history (LVI, age 36).
Almost retired after the Super Bowl win — spent the offseason 'deciding.' Came back.
His sideline meltdowns are mic'd up gold — every other word is bleeped.
Memorized every offensive play in NFL history is the joke about his recall.
headlines: Super Bowl LVI champion.
Hired at 30 — youngest HC in modern NFL.
Architect of the league-wide 11-personnel revolution.
memes: The McVay coaching tree is everywhere — Zac Taylor, Kevin O'Connell, Brandon Staley.
His wife Veronika and the famous 'NFL wives' Instagram era.
His sideline 'GO!' gestures becoming TikTok content.
off_field: Grandson of John McVay (49ers GM in dynasty era).
From Brookhaven, GA — Miami of Ohio alum.
Married Veronika Khomyn in 2022 — the 'McVay wedding' was an NFL social event.
running_jokes: He looks 25 and has been HC since 2017.
His fake jet sweeps fooled DCs for three straight years.
Every Rams loss is followed by 'is McVay quitting?' speculation.

bill-belichick.name: Bill Belichick
drama_notes: 6-time Super Bowl champion HC. The GOAT debate.
Brutal, public divorce from Robert Kraft after 24 years — they reportedly stopped speaking.
Left the Patriots in Jan 2024. Was widely expected to coach the Falcons or Cowboys — got passed over by both. Took the UNC college job in 2024.
Started dating 24-year-old Jordon Hudson in 2024 — became a tabloid story he refuses to address.
headlines: 6 Super Bowls as HC (3 more as DC). All-time most.
333 career wins as HC — passed Don Shula in 2024.
Hired by UNC in late 2024 — first college job.
memes: The hoodie. Always cut at the sleeves.
'We're on to Cincinnati.' Press conference content.
His one-word press conference answers as a permanent meme.
The Jordon Hudson tabloid spread — the internet was not ready.
off_field: From Annapolis, MD — son of a Navy assistant football coach.
Married Debby Clarke (1977-2006); long-term partner Linda Holliday after.
Famously private — doesn't do podcasts, doesn't write books, doesn't give interviews.
running_jokes: He could coach a high school team and make them 12-0.
His Patriots-era cut-off hoodies are now retro-cool.
Refuses to acknowledge any season exists before the next one.

mike-tomlin.name: Mike Tomlin
drama_notes: Has never had a losing season — 17 straight winning records. The streak everyone watches.
Hasn't won a playoff game since the 2016 season — the elephant in every press conference.
Famously loyal — never publicly throws a player under the bus.
His 2024 starting QB carousel (Russell Wilson + Justin Fields) was a national soap opera.
headlines: Super Bowl XLIII champion.
Youngest HC to win a Super Bowl at the time (36).
17 consecutive non-losing seasons — the longest active streak.
memes: The 'Tomlin tunnel' moment vs. Ravens (2013) — kicked Jacoby Jones during a return.
His perfectly cropped pressers — quotable every week.
'The standard is the standard.' On every Steelers wallpaper.
off_field: From Hampton, VA — William & Mary alum.
Married Kiya Winston, three kids.
Mentioned for every college job (USC, Bama) — turns them all down.
running_jokes: The Steelers will go 9-8 every year forever.
He's been on Mt. Rushmore of NFL HCs and never made a Super Bowl in the 2020s.
The 'Tomlin face' after a bad call is a national pastime.

john-harbaugh.name: John Harbaugh
drama_notes: Won Super Bowl XLVII against his brother Jim Harbaugh — the only HarBowl ever.
Has coached the Ravens since 2008 — second-longest active HC tenure.
Famously aggressive on 4th down — the analytics darling of head coaches.
Lamar's 2023 offseason trade-request drama tested the brotherhood — they recovered.
headlines: Super Bowl XLVII champion.
2-time Coach of the Year.
Brothers with Jim Harbaugh (Chargers HC) — only sibling HCs to face in a Super Bowl.
memes: The 'Harbaugh 4th down' decision tree.
His pre-game Catholic prayer ritual is its own piece of NFL lore.
His 'I love my players' speeches becoming NFL Films content.
off_field: From Toledo, OH — Miami of Ohio alum.
Father Jack Harbaugh was a long-time college HC.
Wife Ingrid is the only person who can shut him up.
running_jokes: Every Ravens loss has the same 'we'll be fine' presser.
Going for it on 4th-and-2 from his own 35 = peak Harbaugh.
He'll coach until he's 80.

kyle-shanahan.name: Kyle Shanahan
drama_notes: 0-2 in Super Bowls (LIV vs. Chiefs, LVIII vs. Chiefs) — the 'always blows the lead' narrative.
Was OC for the 28-3 Falcons collapse vs. Patriots — the original Shanahan-loses-leads moment.
Famously turned down the chance to draft Trey Lance — picked Brock Purdy in round 7 instead. Iconic.
Father is Mike Shanahan (2x Super Bowl HC with Broncos).
headlines: 2x Super Bowl appearance HC (LIV, LVIII).
Architect of the modern wide-zone offense.
Brock Purdy from Mr. Irrelevant to MVP candidate is his masterpiece.
memes: The 'Shanahan on the headset' looking devastated face — every loss.
The 28-3 collapse is permanent shorthand for 'don't get comfortable.'
The 'Coach K is mad' Twitter account that screenshots his sideline reactions.
off_field: From Minneapolis, MN — son of Mike Shanahan.
Wife Mandy was his high-school sweetheart.
Friends with Sean McVay (godfather to McVay's kid).
running_jokes: If he's up 17 in the 4th, do not relax.
His QB whisperer rep got real once Purdy hit.
The 'fake jet sweep' is his comfort blanket.

dan-campbell.name: Dan Campbell
drama_notes: Took over the worst franchise in football and made them the most-watched team in the league.
His 'biting kneecaps' opening press conference is the most-quoted intro in NFL history.
Famously aggressive on 4th down — the 'Dan Campbell goes for it again' became a weekly storyline.
Lost the 2024 NFC Championship in heartbreaking fashion — the city of Detroit grieved.
headlines: Took the Lions from 3-13-1 to 15-2 in three years.
First Lions HC to win a playoff game since 1991.
Coach of the Year candidate every year since 2023.
memes: 'We're going to bite a kneecap off' — the most quotable HC speech ever.
His sideline 'LET'S F***ING GO' moments.
The Lions Hard Knocks season turned him into a national hero.
off_field: From Clifton, TX — Texas A&M alum.
Played 11 years in the NFL as a TE.
Wife Holly is the team's unofficial mascot at games.
running_jokes: He'd go for it on 4th-and-22 from his own 4.
Dan Campbell pressers are required viewing.
Detroit will follow him into a war.

gregg-popovich.name: Gregg Popovich
drama_notes: 5x NBA champion HC — the bridge from Tim Duncan to Victor Wembanyama.
Suffered a stroke in November 2024 — Mitch Johnson took over interim duties.
The all-time wins leader in NBA HC history.
Famously wine-obsessed — has a personal cellar that NBA insiders rave about.
headlines: All-time NBA wins leader as HC (1,388+).
USA Basketball Olympic gold (2020 Tokyo).
memes: His 1-word sideline interviews ('we played hard') are legendary content.
The 'Pop drinks wine with everyone' tradition.
His political-commentary pressers cutting through league rules.
off_field: From East Chicago, IN — Air Force Academy grad (rare).
Married Erin Conboy (passed away 2018) — quietly devastated him.
Coached Tim Duncan for 19 years — defined the partnership.
Famously close with David Robinson, Manu Ginobili, Tony Parker.
running_jokes: His sideline reporter interviews are 4 words long.
He's coached every legend you can name and stayed humble.
The Spurs Way is whatever Pop says it is.

erik-spoelstra.name: Erik Spoelstra
drama_notes: 2x NBA champion (2012, 2013) with the LeBron + Wade + Bosh super-team.
Took the no-stars 2023 Heat to the NBA Finals as an 8-seed — generational coaching job.
Riley + Spo + Pat Riley = the Heat's eternal trinity.
Started as the Heat's video coordinator — climbed to HC over 13 years. Pure organizational loyalty story.
headlines: 2x NBA champion HC.
Longest-tenured active NBA HC.
Twice took an 8-seed Heat team to the Finals (2020, 2023).
memes: His timeout-clipboard rage during the playoffs.
The 'Heat Culture' Spo brand is genuinely a phrase he made stick.
His 'we have to play 48 minutes' presser is canon.
off_field: From Evanston, IL — half Filipino, first Filipino-American HC in any major US pro league.
Started as a Heat intern (1995) sorting tape.
Wife Nikki was a former Heat dancer — they have three kids.
running_jokes: Spo will draw up an inbound play that wins a Game 7.
He looks 35 and has been HC since 2008.
Pat Riley's chosen one.

joe-mazzulla.name: Joe Mazzulla
drama_notes: Won NBA title in his second season as HC (2024) at age 35.
Got the Celtics job after Ime Udoka was suspended for a workplace-relationship scandal in 2022.
Famously intense — his pressers are weirdly philosophical and short.
The Tatum/Brown chemistry under Mazzulla is the league's quiet success story.
headlines: 2024 NBA champion.
Youngest active HC in the NBA when promoted.
Ime Udoka's replacement under emergency circumstances.
memes: The 'Joe is reading the Bible courtside' moments.
His weirdly intense Connect-4 references in pressers.
Refusing to acknowledge questions about analytics ('we shoot threes').
off_field: From Johnston, RI — West Virginia alum.
Was a college assistant before the Celtics gave him an assistant gig.
Wife Camai keeps him grounded; two kids.
running_jokes: His three-point obsession is a meme but also wins championships.
He talks about chess, fishing, and the Bible in every press conference.
'Joe Mazzulla Ball' = launch threes, hope they fall.

jj-redick.name: JJ Redick
drama_notes: Hired by the Lakers for 2024-25 season — first HC job ever, came directly from podcasting (Mind the Game with LeBron).
The hire was openly criticized as a 'LeBron pick' — Klutch influence reads as obvious.
His podcast with LeBron literally became the audition tape for the job.
Bronny James drafting in the 2nd round so JJ could coach father + son together added another layer.
headlines: Lakers HC, debut season 2024-25.
15-year NBA career as a sharpshooter (Magic, Clippers, 76ers, Mavericks, Pelicans).
Pivoted from broadcasting to coaching with no prior bench experience.
memes: 'LeBron's podcast guy is now his coach' — the entire NBA Twitter take.
His Duke energy never left — Cameron Crazies fans adopted him as a meme.
The 'JJ thinks he invented basketball' takes from the podcast era.
off_field: From Cookeville, TN — Duke alum (most-hated college player in his era).
Wife Chelsea Kilgore — they have two kids.
Hosts The Old Man and the Three (top-3 NBA podcast).
running_jokes: His first technical foul came in his second game.
Every Lakers loss = 'maybe podcasting was the move.'
The Bronny + LeBron + JJ project is the most-watched experiment of the season.

doc-rivers.name: Doc Rivers
drama_notes: 1x NBA champion (2008 Celtics) — the only ring on his resume.
Has the most playoff series losses with a 3-1 lead in NBA history (4) — the 'Doc blows leads' meme is real.
Took the Bucks job mid-season 2023-24 after Adrian Griffin was fired — turned the team's title hopes upside down.
Famously intense in pressers — has feuds with media members on rotation.
headlines: 2008 NBA champion (Celtics).
All-time top-10 wins HC.
Coached Allen Iverson, Doc-era Celtics, Lob-City Clippers, Embiid 76ers, Giannis Bucks — every era.
memes: 'Doc Rivers blows a 3-1 lead' — the eternal joke.
His son Austin Rivers played for him on the Clippers — the nepotism story of the decade.
His pressers ranting at refs becoming weekly content.
off_field: From Maywood, IL — Marquette alum.
Wife Kris (since 1986); divorced 2017. Three kids — Austin, Spencer, Callie.
Owned a media production company that made the 'Last Dance' Jordan doc.
running_jokes: Up 3-1? He'll find a way.
Every Bucks loss = 'maybe firing Griffin was wrong.'
His timeouts are legendary for the wrong reason.
